alignment_justification: Word-level alignment is chosen because: (1) Hindi is space-delimited, making word boundaries clear; (2) Standard ASR evaluation uses WER (word error rate); (3) Subword/character alignment would add complexity without clear benefit for conversational Hindi evaluations.
lattice_construction: The lattice is built by: (1) Using human reference as backbone; (2) Aligning each model output using DP edit-distance; (3) Adding alternatives at each position from model outputs; (4) Trusting model agreement: 3+ models agreeing on a word overwrites the reference; (5) Accepting spelling variations with >50% character overlap.
trust_model_agreement: If 3 or more models produce the same word at a position, and the human reference has a different word, the model consensus is added to the lattice as a valid alternative. This handles cases where the human reference contains errors.
